title: Turland Hack
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Hack.jpg]] |name=Turland Hack |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' takes place at the same time as the events of {{Film|IV}}, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|II|5|Hack, Turland}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|So you're running your trials today. I wish I could be with you…|Turland Hack to Rookie One|audio=Hack1-RA.flac|}} '''Turland Hack''', known as "Hack," was a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After he failed to become a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Hack took a role in communications and tactical assistance at the Alliance's [[Anchorhead Base]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. There, he helped to train other Rebel pilots, including [[Rookie One]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Toprawa]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces under the command of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] traveled to Tatooine in search of the [[Death Star plans/Legends|stolen plans]] to their [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. Hack was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during a subsequent attack on Anchorhead Base, when Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] destroyed the command center in which he was working. ==Biography== ===Alliance service=== {{Quote|This is Tac-Comm control. Clearance granted. Is that you, kid?''"
"''Hack! Good to hear you.|Turland Hack and Rookie One|}} Turland Hack was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], with the hope of becoming a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. When he proved unable to qualify for flight school, Hack chose to serve the Alliance in communications and tactical assistance. He was assigned to a communications post at [[Anchorhead Base]],''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' manual a training facility near [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], and held the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. Among those who met him, he was simply known as "Hack." [[File:RookieOne1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rookie One in his trials]] While training in Mos Eisley, he met [[Rookie One]], a farmboy from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] who had become a daring civilian pilot. Hack was impressed by the young pilot's skill and enthusiasm for the Rebellion and encouraged him to continue his pilot training with the Alliance. Hack helped tutor Rookie One during his early flights, and served as both a friend and mentor to the young pilot. Shortly after the [[Battle of Toprawa]], Hack was working in [[Tac-Comm control]] on the morning when Rookie One took his trials through the nearby [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]], the culmination of his training on Tatooine. After granting Rookie One's [[T-16 skyhopper/Legends|T-16 skyhopper]] clearance to depart, Hack wished his friend luck, assuring him that Beggar's Canyon was not as difficult as the runs he used to do. Rookie One succeeded in navigating the canyon in close formation with two members of [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]] and engaging target drones, seeing him proceed to [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]] training under [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Jake Farrell/Legends|Jake Farrell]], leaving Hack behind on Tatooine. ===Death at Anchorhead Base=== {{Quote|This is Anchorhead Base… Mayday, we are under attack!''"
"''Hold on, Hack! We're on our way.''"
"''Better hurry, kid. We're in pretty bad shape. I read two, no wait, make that three TIE fighters… They're firing!!|Turland Hack talks to Rookie One, moments before his death|audio=HackRookie-RA.flac|}} [[File:AnchorheadBaseGoBoom.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Turland Hack is killed in the destruction of Anchorhead Base.]] Shortly after Rookie One's trials, the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] arrived at Tatooine, carrying [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] new [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. When [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrived to capture it aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], Organa dispatched the plans to Tatooine in the care of the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. When Vader realized the plans were no longer aboard the ''Tantive IV'', he ordered his forces to begin searching Tatooine.{{Film|IV}} This search eventually led the Imperials to discover Anchorhead Base. The Rebels called for help from [[Kolaador]], where Rookie One was training under Commander [[Ru Murleen]]. Murleen responded, leading Rookie One and [[Thurlow Harris]], in a three-ship [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], to Tatooine. After fighting their way past Imperial forces in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the group proceeded into Tatooine's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] and headed for Anchorhead base. Murleen signaled the base for a status report and Hack responded, telling them an Imperial attack was already underway. Hearing his friend's voice, Rookie One promised to get their as soon as possible—however, it was already too late. While Hack spoke to the approaching Rebels, a group of three [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] entered the canyon and opened fire on Anchorhead Base. Their [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] blasts destroyed the hangar and control center in which Hack was working, killing him instantly. Rookie One and the other Rebels [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|eliminated]] the TIEs responsible for destroying the base and engaged other Imperial forces before they were forced to withdraw. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You shouldn't have any problems; Beggar's Canyon isn't half as tough as the runs we used to do.|Turland Hack|audio=Hack2-RA.flac|}} Hack had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin and was dedicated to the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]]. When he learned that he could not become a pilot himself, he took a role that allowed him to assist in training other pilots and dedicated himself to helping Rookie One become among the best pilots in the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. In this he was successful, and Rookie One went on to fight at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and was instrumental in stopping the Empire's [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] project.''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' However, Hack still wanted to be a pilot himself and wished that he could accompany Rookie One when the young pilot took his trials in Beggar's Canyon. ==Equipment== Hack wore a dark [[Alliance military uniform/Legends|uniform]] and had a [[headset comlink]]. ==Behind the scenes== Turland Hack was created by [[Vincent Lee]] for the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''. The character's appearance was based upon footage of [[Jack Sorensen]], the Director of Business Operations at LucasArts.''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Turland Hack|imagecat=Images of Turland Hack}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Turland Hack }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hack, Turland}} [[Category:Alliance Military officers]] [[Category:Communications officers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Tatooinians]]
